title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Oklahoma
description: Oklahoma voters will elect one U.S. senator in 2026 for the Class II seat. The race matters because it will help determine control of the U.S. Senate, and current race ratings from major forecasters describe Oklahoma as strongly Republican. The contest is also notable because the seat is open following Markwayne Mullin’s resignation in March 2026, and the primary fields include multiple candidates in both parties. Key contrasts so far include Republican candidates emphasizing conservative alignment and experience in office or business, while Democratic candidates are running on grassroots themes such as health care, education, and affordability.
polling_note: No public polling found for this race as of 2026-04-01.

candidates.name: N'Kiyla Thomas
candidates.summary: N'Kiyla Thomas is a Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate in Oklahoma. Her campaign website presents her as a grassroots candidate focused on education, health care, workers, veterans, Native sovereignty, and LGBTQIA+ protections, and she says she is not a career politician. Public biographical coverage is limited, so her broader professional background is not well documented in the sources found.

summary_sources.title: Jasmine Thomas for Oklahoma | Join the Movement Now

career_history.title: U.S. Senate candidate
career_history.description: Running for the Oklahoma U.S. Senate seat in 2026.

candidates.name: Kevin Hern
candidates.summary: Kevin Hern is a Republican candidate for U.S. Senate in Oklahoma and a sitting member of the U.S. House from Oklahoma’s 1st Congressional District. His public profile emphasizes business experience and conservative positions, and he entered the Senate race after Markwayne Mullin resigned from the seat. Hern’s campaign and congressional materials present him as a pro-business, Trump-aligned Republican with prior federal legislative experience.
